message:
fix: complete fastmcp 0.3 + frankensearch 0.3 migrations so mcp_agent_mail_rust builds

My prior asupersync 0.3.0 bump on this workspace (6b433a23) was
premature: it dropped the [patch.crates-io] sibling-path overrides
for asupersync and friends without adjusting the fastmcp version
pins. Because fastmcp-rust 0.2.1 (the current crates.io release)
still depends on asupersync 0.2.9, the published fastmcp transitive
path dragged a second asupersync copy into the graph. That produced
228 "multiple different versions of crate `asupersync`" type
mismatches (FsCx vs fastmcp_rust::Cx) plus ~15 unsized-type errors
in the same compile unit. The fastmcp_rust 0.3 release tag is
pushed but the crates.io publish is still queued behind GH Actions
throttle, so we bridge via a sibling-path patch until it propagates.

Three coupled changes:

1. Cargo.toml [workspace.dependencies]: bump fastmcp workspace
   dep specs from "0.2.0" to "0.3.0" for fastmcp (fastmcp-rust),
   fastmcp-core, fastmcp-server, fastmcp-transport, fastmcp-protocol.
   This is what the local checkout at ../fastmcp_rust is now at
   (per the fastmcp_rust agent's 7a274a4 version bump).

2. Cargo.toml [workspace.dependencies]: bump the frankensearch family
   version pins to match local state — `frankensearch` 0.1.0 → 0.3.0
   (facade), and frankensearch-core/embed/index/fusion 0.1.x → 0.2.0
   (per-crate minor bumps the frankensearch release agent applied).
   The `path = "../frankensearch/..."` resolution fails otherwise
   because path-resolved 0.3.0/0.2.0 doesn't satisfy "^0.1.0".

3. Cargo.toml [patch.crates-io]: temporary sibling-path patch for
   fastmcp-rust + fastmcp-core + fastmcp-server + fastmcp-transport +
   fastmcp-protocol, with an inline comment explaining the block
   should be deleted once fastmcp 0.3 lands on crates.io. Without
   this patch, cargo would still pull fastmcp 0.2.1 from the registry
   (since the new version specs "0.3.0" don't match the 0.2.x
   crates.io artifact), and the build would fail to resolve. The
   path override plus matching version specs makes cargo use the
   sibling checkout, which already uses asupersync 0.3.0 — unifying
   the graph on a single asupersync 0.3.0 entry.

4. crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/lib.rs: three dispatch call sites
   (handle_tools_call, handle_resources_read, handle_prompts_get) gain
   a new trailing `None` argument. fastmcp 0.3 added an optional 8th
   parameter to each — `Option<&BidirectionalSenders>` — to support
   server-initiated bidirectional requests. The MCP dispatch layer
   here doesn't drive any bidirectional flow, so `None` is the
   correct value. Inline comment at each site explains the addition
   so a future reader can wire real bidirectional senders in if
   needed.

- Cargo.lock: refreshed via `cargo update`. Single asupersync 0.3.0
  entry now, fastmcp-rust/core/server/transport/protocol resolve
  from the sibling path.
- `cargo check --workspace` passes clean.
- Unrelated in-flight edit under tests/artifacts/perf/
  archive_batch_100_profile.md from a concurrent profiling run is
  NOT included in this commit.
